summary.what: Add per-repo .timbersignore that extends built-in skip rules
summary.why: The hardcoded default list cannot cover every repo's housekeeping (vendor/, *.lock, custom dep dirs); a configurable extension lets each repo opt-out without code changes while keeping defaults safe and unconfigurable for the common case.
summary.how: New internal/ledger/timbersignore.go reads .timbers/.timbersignore (newline-delimited, # comments) and parses each line with the existing skipRule grammar from Bead 1; rules are loaded once at NewStorage construction and merged with compiledDefaultSkipRules. Filtering plumbs through Storage now so per-repo configuration is honored end-to-end.

notes: Considered TOML for symmetry with potential future config — rejected: would have added a parser dep for one feature, and the .timbersignore name was already telegraphed in the existing storage.go comment. Considered doublestar globs for ** support — rejected: prefix+suffix covers vendor/, *.lock, third_party/ which is the bulk of demand; can layer ** later if real cases emerge. Loader errors fall back to defaults silently because a malformed .timbersignore should never block enforcement — that would invert the gate.
